topTargets.name: Makai Lemon
topTargets.reasoning: At 1.03 with Tate (RSP #1) and Love (RSP #2) almost certainly off the board, Lemon is the cleanest RSP/consensus overlap available — RSP #3 (DoT 88.8, Starter grade, Amon-Ra St. Brown comp) and ADP 11.18. Medium RSP affinity means I trust the board when RSP and ADP agree, and $10.5M cap space comfortably absorbs a 1.03 contract.

topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: RSP #4 (DoT 87.2, Nico Collins comp) and a top-10 ADP target. Size/speed boundary WR profile fits a roster already 7-deep at WR but thin on long-term anchors with 6 contracts expiring. Strong fallback if Lemon goes 1.02.

topTargets.name: Jeremiyah Love
topTargets.reasoning: Long shot at 1.03 (ADP 3.59, RSP #2 with All-Pro upside, Kamara comp) but if Dead Cap Walking surprises at 1.02 with a non-RB, Love is the obvious pivot. RB7 on the roster is misleading — most are flex bodies, not anchors, and Love is a true RB1 building block.

topTargets.name: Fernando Mendoza
topTargets.reasoning: RSP QB1 (DoT 85.6) and ADP 11.39 — value at 1.03 if all three top WRs are gone. Six QBs rostered makes this redundant in a vacuum, but with 6 contracts expiring this year, a long-term QB asset slots in cleanly and trade value at QB1 is real if I don't keep him.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: 2.03 target — RSP #5 (DoT 86.3, Starter grade) and ADP 13.57. Best chance of a top-6 RSP WR sliding into round 2 if the room chases consensus names like Tyson and Mendoza. Slot/flanker versatility plus return-game value is exactly the medium-affinity sweet spot.

capPosture: Comfortable at $34.5M used / $10.5M space — can active-roster all three picks (1.03, 2.03, 3.03) without taxi pressure, though 6 expiring contracts mean I need rookies who can play, not just dev assets.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: 3.03 dart — RSP #11 (DoT 84.4) move TE with QB-convert athletic profile and ADP 21.25 lining up perfectly with the pick slot. TE depth chart is 4 but aging/expiring, and a swing at a developmental TE with Starter-tier upside fits the medium-RSP blend.

summary: Cowboy Up is picking 1.03 with healthy cap and a roster that needs long-term skill anchors as 6 contracts expire. The plan is to take the best WR available — Lemon if Dead Cap Walking goes RB at 1.02, Fields otherwise — then attack value WRs in round 2 (Concepcion) and a developmental upside swing in round 3. Medium RSP affinity steers me toward names where RSP and ADP agree, avoiding pure consensus reaches like Tyson.
